verses.text: A priest must not defile himself for the dead, save for near kin. Set apart. The priest keeps clear of death's defilement.
A priest must not defile himself for the dead, save for near kin. Set apart. The priest keeps clear of death's defilement.
A priest must not defile himself for the dead, save for near kin. Set apart. The priest keeps clear of death's defilement.
A priest must not defile himself for the dead, save for near kin. Set apart. The priest keeps clear of death's defilement.
They are to be holy, not marring beard or flesh. Holy. The priest's body shows his calling.
They are to be holy, not marring beard or flesh. Holy. The priest's body shows his calling.
They marry no harlot or divorced; the priest's daughter who profanes is burnt. Pure house. Holiness extends to the priest's family.
They marry no harlot or divorced; the priest's daughter who profanes is burnt. Pure house. Holiness extends to the priest's family.
They marry no harlot or divorced; the priest's daughter who profanes is burnt. Pure house. Holiness extends to the priest's family.
The high priest mourns for none, nor leaves the sanctuary. Consecrated. He is wholly given to God.
The high priest mourns for none, nor leaves the sanctuary. Consecrated. He is wholly given to God.
The high priest mourns for none, nor leaves the sanctuary. Consecrated. He is wholly given to God.
He marries only a virgin of his own people. His marriage. The priestly line is kept holy.
He marries only a virgin of his own people. His marriage. The priestly line is kept holy.
He marries only a virgin of his own people. His marriage. The priestly line is kept holy.
No priest with a blemish may offer the bread of God. Without blemish. The servant of God must be whole.
No priest with a blemish may offer the bread of God. Without blemish. The servant of God must be whole.
No priest with a blemish may offer the bread of God. Without blemish. The servant of God must be whole.
No priest with a blemish may offer the bread of God. Without blemish. The servant of God must be whole.
No priest with a blemish may offer the bread of God. Without blemish. The servant of God must be whole.
He may eat the holy bread, but not approach the altar. Eats but serves not. Grace feeds even the imperfect.
